name: Route of the Senses
name_original: De Frías a Tobera — circular por Parque natural Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil
description: Hike a moderate 5.2-mile (8.3 km) circular route from Frías to Tobera, exploring historic towns and waterfalls in Montes Obarenes-San Zadorn
region_intro: Hiking around Frías, Spain, offers access to a variety of trails that cater to different fitness levels. The region features a landscape where natural beauty integrates with historical attractions, including viewpoints of the Tobera Waterfalls and the town's hanging houses. These popular hiking trails around Frías range from easy paths suitable for all abilities to more challenging routes requiring good fitness. The terrain often includes scenic vistas and varied elevation, providing diverse outdoor experiences. Best hiking trails around Frías The most popular hiking route is Route of the Senses, a 5.2 miles (8.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 21 minutes to complete, offering varied terrain and scenic points. Another top favourite among local hikers is Frías – Tobera Waterfalls Viewpoint loop from Frías, a difficult 10.4 miles (16.8 km) path. This route provides expansive scenic vistas, including views of the Tobera Waterfalls. Local hikers also love the Frías – Hanging Houses of Frías loop from Frías, a 5.4 miles (8.7 km) trail leading through historical landmarks and natural surroundings, often completed in about 2 hours 35 minutes. Hiking around Frías is defined by historical landmarks, scenic viewpoints, and varied terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more strenuous hikes. The routes in Frías are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 700 hikers have used komoot to explore Frías's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Frías?
A: There are over 20 hiking trails around Frías, offering a diverse range of experiences. These routes cater to various fitness levels, from easy walks to more challenging hikes.

Q: What types of terrain and difficulty levels can I expect on the trails?
A: The trails around Frías feature varied terrain, often combining natural beauty with historical attractions. You'll find options ranging from easy paths suitable for all abilities, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38170445" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tobera Waterfalls Viewpoint – Medieval Bridge of Tobera loop from Frías&lt;/a&gt;, to more strenuous hikes requiring good fitness, like the longer loops that explore the wider landscape.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options in Frías?
A: Yes, Frías offers several easy and family-friendly hiking options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20508644" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Frías – San Vicente Church loop from Frías&lt;/a&gt; is a shorter, gentle walk that explores local historical points. Another accessible route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38170445" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tobera Waterfalls Viewpoint – Medieval Bridge of Tobera loop from Frías&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic views without significant elevation gain.

Q: Can I see waterfalls while hiking in Frías?
A: Yes, the region is known for its waterfalls. You can explore routes that offer scenic vistas of the Tobera Waterfalls, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38170445" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tobera Waterfalls Viewpoint – Medieval Bridge of Tobera loop from Frías&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a closer look at this natural feature.

Q: What historical landmarks or natural features can I explore on the trails?
A: Hiking in Frías allows you to combine natural beauty with historical exploration. Many trails lead past significant landmarks like the Hanging Houses of Frías and the Medieval Bridge of Tobera. You can also discover various mountain passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5580127" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portillo de Busto&lt;/a&gt; or visit historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6365782" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poza de la Sal Salt Flats&lt;/a&gt; nearby.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many of the popular hiking routes around Frías are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38170445" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tobera Waterfalls Viewpoint – Medieval Bridge of Tobera loop from Frías&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20508644" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Frías – San Vicente Church loop from Frías&lt;/a&gt;, which are ideal for exploring the area's highlights.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Frías?
A: The hiking trails in Frías are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the integration of natural scenery with historical sites, and the well-maintained paths that offer diverse outdoor experiences.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Frías?
A: The best time to hike in Frías generally depends on your preference. While the region offers trails year-round, spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summers can be warm, and some trails might be more exposed, so bringing plenty of water is advisable. Winter hiking can offer a unique perspective, especially in areas with higher elevation.

Q: Are there opportunities for wildlife spotting while hiking?
A: While specific wildlife sightings can vary, the natural landscapes around Frías, including areas with chaparral and riparian vegetation, provide habitats for various birds and other local fauna. Keep an eye out, especially in quieter sections of the trails.

Q: Are there any mountain passes to explore near Frías?
A: Yes, the region surrounding Frías features several mountain passes that offer challenging climbs and expansive views. Notable passes include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5580127" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portillo de Busto&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5580144" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climbing to Maíllo de Cubilla&lt;/a&gt;, which are popular among hikers seeking more strenuous ascents.

Q: What are some other interesting places to visit near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the trails, you can explore several fascinating places. Consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6365773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oña Historic Center&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4987028" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Saint Mary of La Hoz&lt;/a&gt; for cultural and historical insights. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6318262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trespaderne&lt;/a&gt; is also nearby, offering additional points of interest.
tips: Beautiful town located on the border between the two provinces and characterized by the castle at the top. From afar you can already see the situation of the nucleus which looks like a postcard.
Old Romanesque bridge that crosses both sides of the Río Molinar and where this construction has been used to cross the riverbank.

The hermitage of the Virgen de la Hoz is located in one of the most charming places in the Las Merindades region, north of Burgos. In the heart of the Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil natural park is this set of hermitages nestled in the rocky cut that the Molinar River has pierced at whim over the millennia.
Viewpoint that is located in the highest part of the town of Tobera and which provides a panoramic view of the town and the valley as a whole.
Another beautiful place in the town of Frías, reminiscent of the hanging houses of Cuenca. The walk is essential.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike this circular route?
faqs.answer: This moderate circular hike, covering approximately 8.34 km, usually takes around 2 hours and 21 minutes to complete. This duration allows time to enjoy the scenic views and points of interest along the way.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features varied terrain. You'll encounter a mix of unpaved paths (about 5.77 km), paved sections (around 1.45 km), and some asphalt (about 695 m). The paths are generally easy to follow, traversing forest tracks and riverside paths, showcasing the rugged relief of the Montes Obarenes.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route is rich in natural beauty and historical landmarks. You'll enjoy a View of Frías Castle and village, the unique Hanging Houses of Frías, and the stunning Tobera Waterfalls and Medieval Bridge. Don't miss the dramatic Hermitage of Saint Mary of La Hoz, nestled in the rock face, and the Tobera Waterfalls Viewpoint.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the hike?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts from Frías. You can find parking available within or near the village, which serves as an excellent base for beginning this circular route.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: While rated as 'moderate', the trail features mostly accessible paths and is generally well-maintained. It requires good fitness but is suitable for a wide range of hikers, including families with some hiking experience. Sure-footedness may be required in some sections.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as it passes through the Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail within the Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park. However, always respect park regulations, such as staying on marked paths and not disturbing wildlife.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, with autumn showcasing beautiful foliage. Summer can be warm, so an early start is advisable. Winter hiking is possible but check weather conditions for potential snow or ice, especially in higher sections.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see in Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park?
faqs.answer: The natural park is a diverse ecological zone. Keep an eye out for various bird species, including griffon vultures soaring overhead. Mammals like wild boar and roe deer also inhabit the area, though they are often more elusive.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the trail?
faqs.answer: Both Frías and Tobera offer options for refreshments and accommodation. Frías, being a historic town, has several cafes, restaurants, and guesthouses where you can enjoy local cuisine and stay overnight. Tobera also has some local establishments.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other trails. Notably, it covers a significant portion of the Ruta de los Sentidos (83%) and also intersects with parts of the Raices de Castilla (38%) and the Ruta de los Monteros del Rey (31%).
